desc: Dali combines highland lakeside scenery, Bai heritage towns, and spring-like temperatures for most of the year, with monsoon timing the main trip-planning factor.
hls: Erhai Lake cycling and village loop routes
Bai minority architecture in Dali Old Town and Xizhou
Great Yunnan produce and tea culture
Mild shoulder seasons suited to slower itineraries

todo.name: Dali Old Town south gate walk
todo.desc: Explore city walls, craft alleys, and temple courtyards in the morning.

todo.name: Erhai west-shore cycle day
todo.desc: Ride between lake villages with photo stops and cafés.

todo.name: Three Pagodas and Chongsheng complex
todo.desc: Visit at opening for quieter grounds and mountain backdrop views.

todo.name: Xizhou Bai village visit
todo.desc: See Bai courtyard homes and try local rushan cheese snacks.

todo.name: Cangshan cableway + trail segment
todo.desc: Use the cableway for alpine views and short ridge walks.

todo.name: Yunnan mushroom hotpot dinner
todo.desc: Book a seasonal mushroom-focused restaurant in town.

wifi.note: Decent in modern hotels, variable in traditional courtyard inns.

prac.fltNote: No nonstop UK/Ireland flights; most routes connect via Kunming, Chengdu, Guangzhou, or Hong Kong before a domestic hop to Dali, with stronger summer seat supply but higher fare pressure.
prac.currency: China local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Monsoon rains (roughly June-September) can interrupt mountain cableway operations with short-notice closures.
Some guesthouses in old lanes are pedestrian-access only; confirm luggage transfer arrangements.
China apps/payments are widely used—set up backup payment methods before arrival.

sweet: Mar-May and Oct-Nov for clear mountain views and comfortable cycling temperatures.
